name: Roads in the center of the island of Ibiza — circular from Sant Antoni de Portmany to Santa Gertrudis
name_original: Carreteras centro de la isla de Ibiza — circular de Sant Antoni de Portmany - Santa Gertrudis
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Sant Antoni De Portmany offer a diverse terrain on the west coast of Ibiza, characterized by coastal stretches, rolling hills, and mountainous ascents. The region features a network of routes that combine continuous sea views with inland trails winding through green fields and almond groves. This Mediterranean landscape provides varied options for road cyclists seeking both challenging climbs and gentler rides. Best road cycling routes around Sant Antoni De Portmany The most popular road cycling route is West coast of Ibiza — circular from Sant Antoni de Portmany, a 44.8 miles (72.1 km) difficult trail that takes 3 hours 48 minutes to complete. This route offers challenging ascents and expansive coastal views. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Roads in the center of the island of Ibiza — circular from Sant Antoni de Portmany to Santa Gertrudis, a moderate 34.4 miles (55.4 km) path. This route explores the island's rural charm and inland scenery. Local road cyclists also love the Sant Antoni de Portmany - Castillo de Ibiza — return via Serra Grossa, a 42.4 miles (68.2 km) trail leading through historical landmarks and offering panoramic views, often completed in about 3 hours 34 minutes. Road cycling around Sant Antoni De Portmany is defined by coastal stretches, green fields, and rolling hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Sant Antoni De Portmany are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 1,400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Sant Antoni De Portmany's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Sant Antoni de Portmany?
A: There are over 50 road cycling routes in the Sant Antoni de Portmany area. These routes cater to various skill levels, including 10 easy, 37 moderate, and 4 difficult options, ensuring a suitable ride for every cyclist.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Sant Antoni de Portmany?
A: The region offers a diverse terrain, from scenic coastal stretches with continuous sea views to inland trails winding through green fields, almond groves, and rolling hills. You'll find both gentle rides and more challenging mountainous ascents, providing a varied cycling experience.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for families in Sant Antoni de Portmany?
A: Yes, Sant Antoni de Portmany offers several easy road cycling routes that are well-suited for families. These often include gentler coastal rides or paths through the authentic rural charm of areas like the Pla de Corona, which is particularly beautiful during the almond blossom season.

Q: Can I bring my dog on road cycling routes in Sant Antoni de Portmany?
A: While road cycling routes are generally on paved roads, which can be challenging for dogs to keep up with, some quieter inland routes might be suitable if your dog is accustomed to running alongside a bike or can be safely carried. Always prioritize your dog's safety and comfort, especially regarding traffic and hot pavement.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available in the area?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Sant Antoni de Portmany are circular, allowing you to start and end your ride in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000780839/west-coast-of-ibiza-circular-from-sant-antoni-de-portmany" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;West coast of Ibiza — circular from Sant Antoni de Portmany&lt;/a&gt;, which offers challenging ascents and expansive coastal views.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints I can see along the road cycling routes?
A: Road cycling routes in the area often pass by stunning natural and historical landmarks. You might encounter the historic Torre de Rovira, enjoy panoramic views from Sa Penya Esbarrada (Heaven's Gate), or pass picturesque churches like those in Santa Agnès. For breathtaking vistas, consider routes that offer views of iconic coasts like Cala Bassa or Cala Gració. You can also explore highlights such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3809407" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ses Torretes Peak&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3309543" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sa Talaia&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Sant Antoni de Portmany?
A: The Mediterranean climate makes Sant Antoni de Portmany ideal for road cycling for much of the year. Spring (March-May) and autumn (September-November) are particularly pleasant, offering mild temperatures and less crowded roads. Winter can also be good, while summer might be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon rides preferable.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for advanced cyclists?
A: Absolutely. For advanced cyclists seeking a challenge, routes with significant elevation gain are available. The Sa Talaia Route, ascending Ibiza's highest mountain, Sa Talaia de Sant Josep, offers a demanding climb with spectacular panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1001484819/sant-antoni-de-portmany-castillo-de-ibiza-return-via-serra-grossa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Antoni de Portmany - Castillo de Ibiza — return via Serra Grossa&lt;/a&gt; route is another difficult option, leading through historical landmarks.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Sant Antoni de Portmany?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 40 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, from continuous sea views to tranquil inland trails, and the well-marked network of routes that cater to all ability levels.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes for beginners?
A: Yes, there are 10 easy road cycling routes around Sant Antoni de Portmany, perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed ride. These routes typically feature gentler gradients and allow you to enjoy the beautiful scenery without excessive effort. Consider exploring the rural charm of the island's center, as seen in routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1001603143/roads-in-the-center-of-the-island-of-ibiza-circular-from-sant-antoni-de-portmany-to-santa-gertrudis" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roads in the center of the island of Ibiza — circular from Sant Antoni de Portmany to Santa Gertrudis&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in Sant Antoni de Portmany?
A: Sant Antoni de Portmany, being a popular tourist destination, offers various parking options. You can typically find public parking areas within the town, often near the port or main attractions. Many routes start directly from the town, making it convenient to park and begin your ride.

Q: Are there cafes or accommodations along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes pass through or near villages and towns where you can find cafes for a refreshment stop. The region also has numerous accommodations that cater to cyclists, offering facilities like bike storage. The popularity of cycling in Sant Antoni means services for cyclists are readily available.

Q: What are some unique natural features to look out for while cycling?
A: Beyond the stunning coastal views, keep an eye out for unique natural features such as the staggered rocky platforms of Punta Galera, perfect for a post-ride dip. Inland, the Pla de Corona offers beautiful almond groves, especially during blossom season, and Es Broll de Buscastell is a hidden oasis with ancient Moorish irrigation channels and lush vegetation.
tips: This church was built at the end of the 18th century. built on a hill between Ibiza and Sant Antoni in a privileged location and offering beautiful views of Ibiza town.
Beautiful road that goes around the mountain crowned by the Talaia de Sant Llorenç. You can see the typical landscape and interior of the island.
Santa Gertrudis is always worth it!

Leave the infamous San Antonio behind you as you climb up and out along the PM-812. The climb swings up through the woods and delivers you above the tree line. Appreciate the smooth road surface, short flat section and epic views back towards the sea before the road kicks up again. Keep pushing on but save a little in reserve as the hill has one final bit of up following the brief downhill rest bite.
